outcome.label: Resolution of Phase Transition Blindness & LLM Integration

nodes.label: ThesisPredictor can achieve differentiable phase transition approximation
nodes.watch_signal: The ability of the linear layer to approximate *arbitrary* step functions effectively across various input ranges.

nodes.label: FiringSquad's differentiability check reliably assesses gradient integrity
nodes.watch_signal: Rectification of the inconsistency between the loss function used for analytical gradient comparison (P_predicted.mean()) and the actual training loss (nn.BCELoss).

nodes.label: JSON output of learned parameters is effectively consumed by LLM
nodes.watch_signal: Observed real-world integration of 'llm_guidance.json' by the LLM Mutator, beyond mere parsing, to influence thesis generation.

nodes.label: Model accurately learns true steepness and threshold of phase transition
nodes.watch_signal: Empirical evidence that a linear layer can converge to the specific steepness and threshold parameters of a non-linear sigmoid, or a revised theoretical justification/architectural change.

nodes.label: Solution generalizes to unseen, complex non-linearities/causal structures
nodes.watch_signal: Successful application of the architecture to real-world datasets with genuinely non-differentiable or highly complex, unmodeled phase transitions and causal chains.
